title: AIA Design Considerations Request
openingLine: Hi, I requested your Design Considerations for Secondary School Music Facilities document. I'm doing some research for the firm — we have a few school projects starting up. Anything else you can share that would help me get up to speed?
customerContext: You're a mid-career architect at a 40-person firm. You're not the principal but you're the one doing the research because the firm just won three K-12 projects. You don't have time for a full sales pitch but you're genuinely curious about good music facility design.

hiddenInfo.reveal: Three new K-12 projects in the next 18 months. Two renovations and one new build.
Most of the team isn't AIA-certified on Wenger content. We do continuing ed about quarterly.
I'm not the spec author on these projects — I support the senior architects. But I shape the research that goes into specs.
We do AIA CES events about once a quarter. Always interested in good content.
Senior architects have used Wenger before. I personally haven't.
Two of the three projects have band, choir, and performance space scope.
We also do athletics work. Lockers and storage come up regularly.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Asked about upcoming firm projects and pipeline
Identified firm's active and future K-12 needs
Researched the firm's prior project history

Solution Framing.behavior: Walked through interior offerings beyond the document

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Proposed a lunch-and-learn to uncover multiple future projects
Discussed firm-wide awareness of Wenger solutions
Offered individual meetings on specific upcoming projects
Probed for athletics/locker scope as adjacent opportunity

BANT.behavior: Identified architects influencing specs (senior architects, contact)
Mapped future project pipeline as the timeline

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Set concrete dates for AIA presentation or lunch-and-learn
Set follow-up tasks tied to upcoming projects

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Offered AIA certification path if not already certified

Other.behavior: Documented in Salesforce under specific projects
